title: NOC 14401 – Storekeepers and Partspersons | GTR Immigration
description: Storekeepers and Partspersons play a critical role in maintaining inventory systems, supplying equipment and replacement parts, and supporting operations across retail, manufacturing, healthcare, and industrial environments in Canada. Classified under NOC 14401, this occupation has seen strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating realistic immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Expansion of industrial production and maintenanceGrowth in construction and infrastructure projectsEntry-level TEER occupation with skilled-trade pathwaysEmployer-supported recruitmentInventory-management modernization
classification.major_group: 144 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations
classification.minor_group: 1440 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations

duties: Storekeepers and Partspersons may perform some or all of the following:
Receive and sort incoming parts and supplies
Store materials in organized warehouse or tool-room systems
Issue parts and supplies for internal use
Maintain inventory records using manual or computerized systems
Prepare requisition orders to replenish stock
Sell replacement parts in retail environments
Advise customers or internal teams on appropriate parts and materials
employment_requirements: Apprenticeship programs or post-secondary training for partspersons
Experience in warehouse or clerical roles (often required)
Optional Red Seal endorsement following successful examination
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 22,700 new positions for Storekeepers and Partspersons, while around 19,700 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Storekeepers and Partspersons are in demand across Canada’s retail, industrial, and logistics sectors. Hiring has accelerated since the pandemic and is projected to remain strong.
Manufacturing plants and warehousesRetail and wholesale operationsMining, forestry, and construction companiesRepair shops and automotive dealersHospitals and public-sector facilities
Annual salaries typically range between $34,700 and $70,900 CAD, depending on province, certification, and experience.
